{{nihongo|'''Maruyama Kofun'''|丸山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]], located in the Jizodō neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Kaizuka, Osaka|Kaizuka, Osaka]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1956.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1794|title=丸山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is also called the {{nihongo|'''Jizo-dō Maruyama Kofun'''|地蔵堂丸山古墳|}}
==Overview== The Maruyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a [[:en:Fluvial terrace|Fluvial terrace]] of the Kogi River, and is orientated to the west. The tumulus has a total length of 73 meters with a 43-meter diameter posterior circular portion. The tumulus was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and had a large number and variety of ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'', including cylindrical, "morning glory-shaped", figurative and house-shaped styles. There was no trace of a moat around the tumulus, and the location and structure of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was not clear. It is believed to have been built around the latter half of the 4th century. Six more smaller ''kofun'' (four {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} and two {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square-type|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}) have been found on the grounds of the nearby Kaizuka Minami Elementary School, forming a cluster of tumuli, suggesting that this was the burial ground for local tribal chieftains during the 4th and 5th centuries.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
[[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] is known to have established a temporary field headquarters on this tumulus in 1585, during his invasion of [[:en:Kii Province|Kii Province]]]. The tumulus (which was then in private hands) was scheduled for demolition for use as landfill in 1952 before a [[:en:Rescue archaeology|Rescue archaeology]] excavation in 1952 placed a stop. The owner donated the site to Kaizuka City in 1957, but a through archaeological excavation was not conducted until 2000 to 2002. In the meantime, the surroundings have been completely developed as a housing estate. The ''haniwa'' excavated from the site were designed a Kaizuka City Tangible Cultural Property in 2006.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a ten-minute walk from [[:en:Izumi-Hashimoto Station|Izumi-Hashimoto Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Hanwa Line|Hanwa Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 72 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 27 meters wide x 4 meters high, 3-tier ;West Constriction: 23 x 13 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 43 meter diameter x 5 meters high, 3-tiers
==Gallery== <gallery> File:Maruyama Kofun (Kaizuka), funcho.jpg|Panoramic view File:Maruyama Kofun (Kaizuka), kouenbu.jpg|Posterior from the anterior File:Maruyama Kofun (Kaizuka), zenpoubu.jpg|Anterior from the posterior 地蔵堂古墳群 出土品.JPG|excavated haniwa </gallery>

It is located on a [[:en:Fluvial terrace|Fluvial terrace]] of the Kogi River, and is orientated to the west. The tumulus has a total length of 73 meters with a 43-meter diameter posterior circular portion. The tumulus was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and had a large number and variety of ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'', including cylindrical, "morning glory-shaped", figurative and house-shaped styles. There was no trace of a moat around the tumulus, and the location and structure of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was not clear. It is believed to have been built around the latter half of the 4th century. Six more smaller ''kofun'' (four {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} and two {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square-type|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}) have been found on the grounds of the nearby Kaizuka Minami Elementary School, forming a cluster of tumuli, suggesting that this was the burial ground for local tribal chieftains during the 4th and 5th centuries.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
[[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] is known to have established a temporary field headquarters on this tumulus in 1585, during his invasion of [[:en:Kii Province|Kii Province]]]. The tumulus (which was then in private hands) was scheduled for demolition for use as landfill in 1952 before a [[:en:Rescue archaeology|Rescue archaeology]] excavation in 1952 placed a stop. The owner donated the site to Kaizuka City in 1957, but a through archaeological excavation was not conducted until 2000 to 2002. In the meantime, the surroundings have been completely developed as a housing estate. The ''haniwa'' excavated from the site were designed a Kaizuka City Tangible Cultural Property in 2006.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a ten-minute walk from [[:en:Izumi-Hashimoto Station|Izumi-Hashimoto Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Hanwa Line|Hanwa Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 72 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 27 meters wide x 4 meters high, 3-tier ;West Constriction: 23 x 13 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 43 meter diameter x 5 meters high, 3-tiers
==Gallery== <gallery> File:Maruyama Kofun (Kaizuka), funcho.jpg|Panoramic view File:Maruyama Kofun (Kaizuka), kouenbu.jpg|Posterior from the anterior File:Maruyama Kofun (Kaizuka), zenpoubu.jpg|Anterior from the posterior 地蔵堂古墳群 出土品.JPG|excavated haniwa </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Osaka)]]

the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=keyhole-shaped Kofun}}, located in the Jizodō neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Kaizuka, Osaka|en|Kaizuka, Osaka|WD=Q908972|lt=Kaizuka, Osaka}} in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1956.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1794|title=丸山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is also called the {{nihongo|'''Jizo-dō Maruyama Kofun'''|地蔵堂丸山古墳|}}
==Overview== The Maruyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a {{ill|Fluvial terrace|en|Fluvial terrace|WD=Q1069618|lt=Fluvial terrace}} of the Kogi River, and is orientated to the west. The tumulus has a total length of 73 meters with a 43-meter diameter posterior circular portion. The tumulus was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and had a large number and variety of ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'', including cylindrical, "morning glory-shaped", figurative and house-shaped styles. There was no trace of a moat around the tumulus, and the location and structure of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was not clear. It is believed to have been built around the latter half of the 4th century. Six more smaller ''kofun'' (four {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} and two {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square-type|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}) have been found on the grounds of the nearby Kaizuka Minami Elementary School, forming a cluster of tumuli, suggesting that this was the burial ground for local tribal chieftains during the 4th and 5th centuries.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
{{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} is known to have established a temporary field headquarters on this tumulus in 1585, during his invasion of {{ill|Kii Province|en|Kii Province|WD=Q864053|lt=Kii Province}}]. The tumulus (which was then in private hands) was scheduled for demolition for use as landfill in 1952 before a {{ill|Rescue archaeology|en|Rescue archaeology|WD=Q653838|lt=Rescue archaeology}} excavation in 1952 placed a stop. The owner donated the site to Kaizuka City in 1957, but a through archaeological excavation was not conducted until 2000 to 2002. In the meantime, the surroundings have been completely developed as a housing estate. The ''haniwa'' excavated from the site were designed a Kaizuka City Tangible Cultural Property in 2006.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a ten-minute walk from {{ill|Izumi-Hashimoto Station|en|Izumi-Hashimoto Station|WD=Q898395|lt=Izumi-Hashimoto Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Hanwa Line|en|Hanwa Line|WD=Q1154800|lt=Hanwa Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 72 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 27 meters wide x 4 meters high, 3-tier ;West Constriction: 23 x 13 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 43 meter diameter x 5 meters high, 3-tiers
==Gallery== <gallery> File:Maruyama Kofun (Kaizuka), funcho.jpg|Panoramic view File:Maruyama Kofun (Kaizuka), kouenbu.jpg|Posterior from the anterior File:Maruyama Kofun (Kaizuka), zenpoubu.jpg|Anterior from the posterior 地蔵堂古墳群 出土品.JPG|excavated haniwa </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Osaka)]]

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Kaizuka, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} | name =Maruyama Kofun | native_name =丸山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Maruyama Kofun (Kaizuka), funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Maruyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Maruyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.428597|N|135.355361|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Kaizuka, Osaka|en|Kaizuka, Osaka|WD=Q908972|lt=Kaizuka, Osaka}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|25|42.95|N|135|21|19|E}}}}}} {{nihongo|'''Maruyama Kofun'''|丸山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=keyhole-shaped Kofun}}, located in the Jizodō neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Kaizuka, Osaka|en|Kaizuka, Osaka|WD=Q908972|lt=Kaizuka, Osaka}} in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1956.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1794|title=丸山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is also called the {{nihongo|'''Jizo-dō Maruyama Kofun'''|地蔵堂丸山古墳|}}
==Overview== The Maruyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a {{ill|Fluvial terrace|en|Fluvial terrace|WD=Q1069618|lt=Fluvial terrace}} of the Kogi River, and is orientated to the west. The tumulus has a total length of 73 meters with a 43-meter diameter posterior circular portion. The tumulus was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and had a large number and variety of ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'', including cylindrical, "morning glory-shaped", figurative and house-shaped styles. There was no trace of a moat around the tumulus, and the location and structure of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was not clear. It is believed to have been built around the latter half of the 4th century. Six more smaller ''kofun'' (four {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} and two {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square-type|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}) have been found on the grounds of the nearby Kaizuka Minami Elementary School, forming a cluster of tumuli, suggesting that this was the burial ground for local tribal chieftains during the 4th and 5th centuries.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
{{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} is known to have established a temporary field headquarters on this tumulus in 1585, during his invasion of {{ill|Kii Province|en|Kii Province|WD=Q864053|lt=Kii Province}}]. The tumulus (which was then in private hands) was scheduled for demolition for use as landfill in 1952 before a {{ill|Rescue archaeology|en|Rescue archaeology|WD=Q653838|lt=Rescue archaeology}} excavation in 1952 placed a stop. The owner donated the site to Kaizuka City in 1957, but a through archaeological excavation was not conducted until 2000 to 2002. In the meantime, the surroundings have been completely developed as a housing estate. The ''haniwa'' excavated from the site were designed a Kaizuka City Tangible Cultural Property in 2006.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a ten-minute walk from {{ill|Izumi-Hashimoto Station|en|Izumi-Hashimoto Station|WD=Q898395|lt=Izumi-Hashimoto Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Hanwa Line|en|Hanwa Line|WD=Q1154800|lt=Hanwa Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 72 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 27 meters wide x 4 meters high, 3-tier ;West Constriction: 23 x 13 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 43 meter diameter x 5 meters high, 3-tiers
==Gallery== <gallery> File:Maruyama Kofun (Kaizuka), funcho.jpg|Panoramic view File:Maruyama Kofun (Kaizuka), kouenbu.jpg|Posterior from the anterior File:Maruyama Kofun (Kaizuka), zenpoubu.jpg|Anterior from the posterior 地蔵堂古墳群 出土品.JPG|excavated haniwa </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Osaka)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons category-inline|Maruyama Kofun (Kaizuka)}} *[https://www.city.kaizuka.lg.jp/bunkazai/bunkazaidata/bunkazai/kuni_sitei/siseki/maruyamakohun.html Kaizuka City home page]

| map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Kaizuka, Osaka|en|Kaizuka, Osaka|WD=Q908972|lt=Kaizuka, Osaka|1=Kaizuka, Osaka}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|25|42.95|N|135|21|19|E}}}}}} {{nihongo|'''Maruyama Kofun'''|丸山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=keyhole-shaped Kofun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}, located in the Jizodō neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Kaizuka, Osaka|en|Kaizuka, Osaka|WD=Q908972|lt=Kaizuka, Osaka|1=Kaizuka, Osaka}} in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1956.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1794|title=丸山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is also called the {{nihongo|'''Jizo-dō Maruyama Kofun'''|地蔵堂丸山古墳|}}
==Overview== The Maruyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a {{ill|Fluvial terrace|en|Fluvial terrace|WD=Q1069618|lt=Fluvial terrace|1=Fluvial terrace}} of the Kogi River, and is orientated to the west. The tumulus has a total length of 73 meters with a 43-meter diameter posterior circular portion. The tumulus was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and had a large number and variety of ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'', including cylindrical, "morning glory-shaped", figurative and house-shaped styles. There was no trace of a moat around the tumulus, and the location and structure of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was not clear. It is believed to have been built around the latter half of the 4th century. Six more smaller ''kofun'' (four {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} and two {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square-type|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}) have been found on the grounds of the nearby Kaizuka Minami Elementary School, forming a cluster of tumuli, suggesting that this was the burial ground for local tribal chieftains during the 4th and 5th centuries.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
{{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} is known to have established a temporary field headquarters on this tumulus in 1585, during his invasion of {{ill|Kii Province|en|Kii Province|WD=Q864053|lt=Kii Province|1=Kii Province}}]. The tumulus (which was then in private hands) was scheduled for demolition for use as landfill in 1952 before a {{ill|Rescue archaeology|en|Rescue archaeology|WD=Q653838|lt=Rescue archaeology|1=Rescue archaeology}} excavation in 1952 placed a stop. The owner donated the site to Kaizuka City in 1957, but a through archaeological excavation was not conducted until 2000 to 2002. In the meantime, the surroundings have been completely developed as a housing estate. The ''haniwa'' excavated from the site were designed a Kaizuka City Tangible Cultural Property in 2006.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a ten-minute walk from {{ill|Izumi-Hashimoto Station|en|Izumi-Hashimoto Station|WD=Q898395|lt=Izumi-Hashimoto Station|1=Izumi-Hashimoto Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Hanwa Line|en|Hanwa Line|WD=Q1154800|lt=Hanwa Line|1=Hanwa Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 72 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 27 meters wide x 4 meters high, 3-tier ;West Constriction: 23 x 13 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 43 meter diameter x 5 meters high, 3-tiers

In the meantime, the surroundings have been completely developed as a housing estate. The ''haniwa'' excavated from the site were designed a Kaizuka City Tangible Cultural Property in 2006.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a ten-minute walk from {{ill|Izumi-Hashimoto Station|en|Izumi-Hashimoto Station|WD=Q898395|lt=Izumi-Hashimoto Station|1=Izumi-Hashimoto Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Hanwa Line|en|Hanwa Line|WD=Q1154800|lt=Hanwa Line|1=Hanwa Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 72 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 27 meters wide x 4 meters high, 3-tier ;West Constriction: 23 x 13 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 43 meter diameter x 5 meters high, 3-tiers

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Kaizuka, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} | name =Maruyama Kofun | native_name =丸山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Maruyama Kofun (Kaizuka), funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Maruyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Maruyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.428597|N|135.355361|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Kaizuka, Osaka|en|Kaizuka, Osaka|WD=Q908972|lt=Kaizuka, Osaka|1=Kaizuka, Osaka}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|25|42.95|N|135|21|19|E}}}}}} {{nihongo|'''Maruyama Kofun'''|丸山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=keyhole-shaped Kofun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}, located in the Jizodō neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Kaizuka, Osaka|en|Kaizuka, Osaka|WD=Q908972|lt=Kaizuka, Osaka|1=Kaizuka, Osaka}} in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1956.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1794|title=丸山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is also called the {{nihongo|'''Jizo-dō Maruyama Kofun'''|地蔵堂丸山古墳|}}
==Overview== The Maruyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a {{ill|Fluvial terrace|en|Fluvial terrace|WD=Q1069618|lt=Fluvial terrace|1=Fluvial terrace}} of the Kogi River, and is orientated to the west. The tumulus has a total length of 73 meters with a 43-meter diameter posterior circular portion. The tumulus was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and had a large number and variety of ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'', including cylindrical, "morning glory-shaped", figurative and house-shaped styles. There was no trace of a moat around the tumulus, and the location and structure of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was not clear. It is believed to have been built around the latter half of the 4th century. Six more smaller ''kofun'' (four {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} and two {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square-type|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}) have been found on the grounds of the nearby Kaizuka Minami Elementary School, forming a cluster of tumuli, suggesting that this was the burial ground for local tribal chieftains during the 4th and 5th centuries.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
{{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} is known to have established a temporary field headquarters on this tumulus in 1585, during his invasion of {{ill|Kii Province|en|Kii Province|WD=Q864053|lt=Kii Province|1=Kii Province}}]. The tumulus (which was then in private hands) was scheduled for demolition for use as landfill in 1952 before a {{ill|Rescue archaeology|en|Rescue archaeology|WD=Q653838|lt=Rescue archaeology|1=Rescue archaeology}} excavation in 1952 placed a stop. The owner donated the site to Kaizuka City in 1957, but a through archaeological excavation was not conducted until 2000 to 2002. In the meantime, the surroundings have been completely developed as a housing estate. The ''haniwa'' excavated from the site were designed a Kaizuka City Tangible Cultural Property in 2006.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a ten-minute walk from {{ill|Izumi-Hashimoto Station|en|Izumi-Hashimoto Station|WD=Q898395|lt=Izumi-Hashimoto Station|1=Izumi-Hashimoto Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Hanwa Line|en|Hanwa Line|WD=Q1154800|lt=Hanwa Line|1=Hanwa Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 72 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 27 meters wide x 4 meters high, 3-tier ;West Constriction: 23 x 13 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 43 meter diameter x 5 meters high, 3-tiers
==Gallery== <gallery> File:Maruyama Kofun (Kaizuka), funcho.jpg|Panoramic view File:Maruyama Kofun (Kaizuka), kouenbu.jpg|Posterior from the anterior File:Maruyama Kofun (Kaizuka), zenpoubu.jpg|Anterior from the posterior 地蔵堂古墳群 出土品.JPG|excavated haniwa </gallery>

There was no trace of a moat around the tumulus, and the location and structure of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was not clear. It is believed to have been built around the latter half of the 4th century. Six more smaller ''kofun'' (four {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} and two {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square-type|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}) have been found on the grounds of the nearby Kaizuka Minami Elementary School, forming a cluster of tumuli, suggesting that this was the burial ground for local tribal chieftains during the 4th and 5th centuries.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
{{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} is known to have established a temporary field headquarters on this tumulus in 1585, during his invasion of {{ill|Kii Province|en|Kii Province|WD=Q864053|lt=Kii Province|1=Kii Province}}]. The tumulus (which was then in private hands) was scheduled for demolition for use as landfill in 1952 before a {{ill|Rescue archaeology|en|Rescue archaeology|WD=Q653838|lt=Rescue archaeology|1=Rescue archaeology}} excavation in 1952 placed a stop. The owner donated the site to Kaizuka City in 1957, but a through archaeological excavation was not conducted until 2000 to 2002. In the meantime, the surroundings have been completely developed as a housing estate. The ''haniwa'' excavated from the site were designed a Kaizuka City Tangible Cultural Property in 2006.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a ten-minute walk from {{ill|Izumi-Hashimoto Station|en|Izumi-Hashimoto Station|WD=Q898395|lt=Izumi-Hashimoto Station|1=Izumi-Hashimoto Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Hanwa Line|en|Hanwa Line|WD=Q1154800|lt=Hanwa Line|1=Hanwa Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 72 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 27 meters wide x 4 meters high, 3-tier ;West Constriction: 23 x 13 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 43 meter diameter x 5 meters high, 3-tiers
==Gallery== <gallery> File:Maruyama Kofun (Kaizuka), funcho.jpg|Panoramic view File:Maruyama Kofun (Kaizuka), kouenbu.jpg|Posterior from the anterior File:Maruyama Kofun (Kaizuka), zenpoubu.jpg|Anterior from the posterior 地蔵堂古墳群 出土品.JPG|excavated haniwa </gallery>

The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1956.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1794|title=丸山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is also called the {{nihongo|'''Jizo-dō Maruyama Kofun'''|地蔵堂丸山古墳|}}
==Overview== The Maruyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a {{ill|Fluvial terrace|en|Fluvial terrace|WD=Q1069618|lt=Fluvial terrace|1=Fluvial terrace}} of the Kogi River, and is orientated to the west. The tumulus has a total length of 73 meters with a 43-meter diameter posterior circular portion. Six more smaller ''kofun'' (four {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} and two {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square-type|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}) have been found on the grounds of the nearby Kaizuka Minami Elementary School, forming a cluster of tumuli, suggesting that this was the burial ground for local tribal chieftains during the 4th and 5th centuries.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

The owner donated the site to Kaizuka City in 1957, but a through archaeological excavation was not conducted until 2000 to 2002. In the meantime, the surroundings have been completely developed as a housing estate. The ''haniwa'' excavated from the site were designed a Kaizuka City Tangible Cultural Property in 2006.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1956.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1794|title=丸山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is also called the {{nihongo|'''Jizo-dō Maruyama Kofun'''|地蔵堂丸山古墳|}}
==Overview== The Maruyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpōkōenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpōkōenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpōkōenfun|1=[[Zenpōkōenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a {{ill|Fluvial terrace|en|Fluvial terrace|qid=Q1069618|lt=Fluvial terrace|1=Fluvial terrace}} of the Kogi River, and is orientated to the west. The tumulus has a total length of 73 meters with a 43-meter diameter posterior circular portion. The tumulus was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and had a large number and variety of ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'', including cylindrical, "morning glory-shaped", figurative and house-shaped styles. There was no trace of a moat around the tumulus, and the location and structure of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was not clear. It is believed to have been built around the latter half of the 4th century. Six more smaller ''kofun'' (four {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} and two {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square-type|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}) have been found on the grounds of the nearby Kaizuka Minami Elementary School, forming a cluster of tumuli, suggesting that this was the burial ground for local tribal chieftains during the 4th and 5th centuries.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
{{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qid=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} is known to have established a temporary field headquarters on this tumulus in 1585, during his invasion of {{ill|Kii Province|en|Kii Province|qid=Q864053|lt=Kii Province|1=Kii Province}}]. The tumulus (which was then in private hands) was scheduled for demolition for use as landfill in 1952 before a {{ill|Rescue archaeology|en|Rescue archaeology|qid=Q653838|lt=Rescue archaeology|1=Rescue archaeology}} excavation in 1952 placed a stop. The owner donated the site to Kaizuka City in 1957, but a through archaeological excavation was not conducted until 2000 to 2002. In the meantime, the surroundings have been completely developed as a housing estate. The ''haniwa'' excavated from the site were designed a Kaizuka City Tangible Cultural Property in 2006.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a ten-minute walk from {{ill|Izumi-Hashimoto Station|en|Izumi-Hashimoto Station|qid=Q898395|lt=Izumi-Hashimoto Station|1=Izumi-Hashimoto Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|qid=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Hanwa Line|en|Hanwa Line|qid=Q1154800|lt=Hanwa Line|1=Hanwa Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 72 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 27 meters wide x 4 meters high, 3-tier ;West Constriction: 23 x 13 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 43 meter diameter x 5 meters high, 3-tiers
==Gallery== <gallery> File:Maruyama Kofun (Kaizuka), funcho.jpg|Panoramic view File:Maruyama Kofun (Kaizuka), kouenbu.jpg|Posterior from the anterior File:Maruyama Kofun (Kaizuka), zenpoubu.jpg|Anterior from the posterior 地蔵堂古墳群 出土品.JPG|excavated haniwa </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Osaka)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons category-inline|Maruyama Kofun (Kaizuka)}} *[https://www.city.kaizuka.lg.jp/bunkazai/bunkazaidata/bunkazai/kuni_sitei/siseki/maruyamakohun.html Kaizuka City home page]
